sutta: the wanderer came to see the exalted one and paid respect etc then as he said to one side vacha gota the wanderer said this to the exalted one i've heard it said master gautama that gotama the recluse speaks thus arms should be given to me not to others to my followers not to another's followers arms given to me are of great fruit not so those given to others arms given to my followers are of great prophet not arms given to another's followers now master gautama do they who say these things correctly repeat the views of the worthy gotama without misrepresenting him by uttering an untruth do they expound their views in accordance with his teaching so that one who is of his doctrine of his way of thinking may not give grounds for reproach in stating it indeed we are anxious not to misrepresent the worthy goshama and the buddha replied those who say so vacha are not of my way of thinking moreover such misrepresents me by stating what is not true but a falsehood indeed bacha who so prevents another from giving arms obstructs him in three ways robs him of three things what three first he obstructs the giver in acquiring merit second he prevents the receivers from getting a gift third his own self is already ruined utterly ruined who so prevents another from giving arms bacha him in three ways robs him of three things but this is what i really do declare if one should throw away spot pot scourings or the rinsings of cups into a pool or says pit that means a pit of excrement even with the idea of feeding the creatures that live therein i declare it would be a source of merit to him to say nothing of his feeding beings that are human nevertheless i say vacha that a gift given in the case of the virtuous is of great fruit not those given in the case of the wicked by virtuous i mean one who has abandoned five qualities and possesses five qualities what are the five qualities he has abandoned sensual desire is abandoned malevolence sloth and torpor excitement and blurry doubt and wavering are abandoned these are the five and of what five qualities is he possessed he possesses the constituents of morality possessed by the adept the constituents of concentration wisdom liberation knowledge and vision of liberation he has these five qualities thus when five qualities are abandoned and five possessed a gift is of great fruit
commentary: this is uh interesting subta because here somebody is asking the buddha whether he actually said that offerings should be made to him and his followers not to some other other external sec followers like and the buddha denied it the buddha said he never said that and i said if anybody says such a thing then [Music] in three ways he has done great wrong and the buddha also says that even the crumbs are the leftover of food now even you throw into a pool or a bit of excrement with the idea of feeding the creatures there even then also you get some merit not to not to talk about feeding human beings and then lastly the buddha says that if you give to an arahant one who has abandoned the five hindrances and possessed of five other qualities of the arahant then you would have great merit [Music] now um one thing i like to remark here is that one of the wrong livelihoods for one of the wrong ways of livelihood for a manga is to belittle another monk personally and to talk bad and about another monk and discourage uh lay people from making offerings to another monk and that is very bad because here you can see that the buddha is saying even those external sex people even then also he does not say that you should not make offerings to them even external sex people even with wrong views and the buddha in fact there's one suta where the followers of a jain said i think mikantas after they left the nigantas and came to follow the buddha the buddha still advised them to continue giving to those niganta and so so you can see here that anyone practicing the spiritual path and we should not be little others even though they are i mean not to the extent of asking people not to make offerings to these other people and
